verses.text: We turned into the wilderness by the Red sea. The LORD: ye have compassed this mountain long enough — turn northward. Pass through Esau's coast in Seir; meddle not, I have given mount Seir unto Esau. Buy meat and water for money. The LORD thy God hath blessed thee, knoweth thy walking through this great wilderness; these forty years He hath been with thee; thou hast lacked nothing. Guidance: God's mercy is fair on all sides. Edom is Esau's, given long ago — Israel pays for what they take through their brother's country. 'Thou hast lacked nothing' is the gold of the chapter — forty years of provision summed up in five words.

We passed by from our brethren of Esau, by Elath, Ezion-gaber; turned through the wilderness of Moab. The LORD: distress not the Moabites, nor contend with them in battle: I will not give thee of their land; I have given Ar unto the children of Lot. Guidance: Moab too is off-limits — Lot's line, kept under God's hand even though their forefather drifted away centuries ago. God remembers ancestries Israel might prefer to forget. Mercy reaches further back than memory.

Mercy reaches further back than memory.

The Emims dwelt there in times past, a people great and tall as Anakims. The Horims dwelt in Seir before, but Esau's children succeeded them. The space from Kadesh-barnea to the brook Zered was thirty-eight years — until all the men of war were wasted out, as the LORD sware. The hand of the LORD was against them until they were consumed. Guidance: Giants used to live where ordinary people now live. Empires shrink and bloodlines fade. Thirty-eight years for one generation to die out — patience disguised as punishment. God lets time do what swords would not need to.

When all the men of war were dead, the LORD: pass over Ar, the coast of Moab; when ye come nigh Ammon, distress them not — I have given it unto the children of Lot. That also was a land of giants — Zamzummims, tall as Anakims; but the LORD destroyed them. The Caphtorims destroyed the Avims, dwelt in their stead. Guidance: Two nations God will NOT let Israel touch — both Lot's. And in the same breath, He records that He has been quietly rearranging the map for everyone, giants included. The Lord of armies is also the Lord of demographics. He decides who lives where, and how long.

The LORD: rise, pass over Arnon: I have given into thine hand Sihon the Amorite. This day will I begin to put dread of thee upon the nations. I sent messengers to Sihon: let me pass. He would not — the LORD hardened his spirit, that He might deliver him. Behold, I have begun to give Sihon and his land. Guidance: The boundary changes. Esau, Moab, Ammon — protected. Sihon the Amorite — given. The same God who said 'meddle not' now says 'begin.' Reading God's heart for one nation does not equal reading His heart for another. Each people stands under its own clock.

Sihon came out against us at Jahaz. The LORD delivered him; we smote him, his sons, all his people. We took all his cities, utterly destroyed the men, women, little ones; only the cattle we took for prey. From Aroer on the Arnon, to Gilead, the LORD our God delivered all unto us. Only unto Ammon thou camest not, whatsoever the LORD our God forbade us. Guidance: The sword does what God commands and stops where God says stop. Israel doesn't push past the line; the cities of Ammon stay untouched because the LORD said so. Obedience is also what you don't take.
